Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks for vendor bills related to Heartland Movers and Swift Relocations to find current vendor charges across client accounts. Agent discovers the Heartland Q1 bill ($12,800 with $12,800 payment hold) and the breakdown of charges per move.
OE 2: Search Airtable Relocations table for all records to find which vendor handled each client move and the vendor cost listed in Special Requirements. Agent discovers vendor assignments (Heartland vs. Swift) per relocation and per-move vendor costs ($1,600 standard per Heartland move). Also discovers the cancelled moves. Lily Marchetti (cancelled Feb 10, Heartland never acknowledged) and Danielle Osei (cancelled Feb 13, Heartland never acknowledged) and Priya Venkatesh (completed by Swift, not Heartland), which are the core of the billing dispute.
OE 3: Search emails for Heartland billing dispute correspondence (queries like "Heartland", "dispute", "overbilling") to find Marcus's formal dispute letter to Jake Loomis, Chloe's cancellation evidence, and the payment hold status. Agent discovers the $4,800 overcharge for 3 illegitimate line items and that the dispute is still unresolved.
OE 4: Search Slack messages for vendor rate increase discussions (queries like "rate increase", "Heartland", "Swift", "vendor") to find: Greg Pallone's Swift 15% base rate increase + $500 DOT documentation surcharge announcement (effective immediately, April 11), Jake Loomis/Heartland tying their 20% Q3 rate increase to the unresolved HM-2026-Q1-0042 billing dispute, Chloe probing Lakeline Logistics as a backup vendor (limited to Midwest and Southeast corridors — cannot serve as national replacement), and Blessing reporting Swift driver pushback on new paperwork. Agent also discovers Alejandro's own margin compression model showing 6-8 point compression.
OE 5: Search Slack #finance channel (via conversations_history or conversations_search_messages) for financial discussions to find: Hana's DOT penalty accrual entry ($50,000 contingent liability, ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001), Marcus's post about the Mosaic prototype damage ($90K value, $50K insurance coverage, $40K gap), Hana's Mosaic credit memo (CM-2026-0415), Marcus's KeyMove piano insurance claim ($1,200 rider), and Marcus's note about the StormCloud $500 credit posted to the wrong QuickBooks estimate.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for invoices, accounts, and accruals to verify the booked amounts. Agent discovers: DOT penalty accrual ($50,000 in Regulatory Compliance DOT Penalties account), Mosaic damage liability accrual ($90,000 contingent), stipend reimbursement holds (STIP-2026-0089 Kevin Tran $89 already paid, STIP-2026-0094 Andre Washington $89 on hold, STIP-2026-0097 Jordan Ekwueme $45 on hold), and the StormCloud estimate where the $500 goodwill credit was misposted.
OE 7: Search emails for the stipend fraud investigation (queries like "stipend", "duplicate receipt", "TechComfort") to find Dmitri's audit findings email to Marcus detailing three flagged transactions ($89 duplicate hash Kevin Tran/Andre Washington, $45 PDF template match Jordan Ekwueme), Marcus's reply confirming QuickBooks payment statuses and holds placed, and the verification request emails sent to client HR contacts (Mina to Rachel Whitfield for Vectral, Emeka to Tamara Byrd for Mosaic and Tessa Moreno for BrightLoop).
OE 8: Search CRM for deals across active client companies (via crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies) to find contract values and deal amounts per client. Agent discovers deal values needed for margin calculation: Vectral (~$109,900 total, 32% margin), NorthWind ($60,000 enterprise contract, $14,700 Denver expansion), Canopy Health (multiple deals including $9,210 Q2 wave and $12,170 executive relocation), Mosaic ($9,010 April batch), BrightLoop, GreenStack, etc.
OE 9: Search Slack messages for AM exposure mapping responses (queries like "exposure", "client impact", "rate increase") to find what each AM reported after David's request. Agent discovers: Mina flagged Vectral and BrightLoop as most exposed, Emeka flagged Mosaic, and Catalina did not respond (went silent).
OE 10: Search Slack for Hana's reconciliation findings beyond the original Heartland dispute to find that she discovered broader reconciliation gaps during the vendor consolidation crisis (referenced in her persona brief and Slack posts in #finance).
OE 11: Verify and update QuickBooks accrual amounts (via quickbooks_update_account) if any booked amounts don't match the actual current status discovered during investigation e.g., adjusting the Heartland dispute accrual if Hana's additional findings change the disputed total, or updating the DOT accrual if the documentation audit resolved favorably.
OE 12: Look up Marcus Thorne's contact details using contacts_search_contacts to confirm his email address (marcus.thorne@moveops.com) for the email delivery.
OE 13: Post a financial exposure summary in the #finance Slack channel (via conversations_add_message) covering: vendor margin impact analysis by client, total contingent liability rollup across all open items (Heartland dispute, DOT accrual, Mosaic damage, stipend holds, StormCloud credit error, KeyMove piano claim), and Heartland dispute status so Marcus and Hana can validate the numbers.
OE 14: Update Linear tickets tracking financial items (via linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment) with current financial context e.g., the Heartland dispute tracking ticket, DOT compliance tickets, Mosaic damage claim ticket, and stipend investigation ticket (ENG-220).
OE 15: Send email (via send_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com with the full margin analysis (current vendor costs per client, projected margin compression from rate increases, clients most impacted), total contingent liability rollup with verified amounts, and Heartland dispute findings giving Marcus everything he needs for Elena's vendor strategy memo.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks for vendor bills related to Heartland Movers and Swift Relocations to find current vendor charges across client accounts. Agent discovers the Heartland Q1 bill ($12,800 with $12,800 payment hold) and the breakdown of charges per move.
OE 2: Search Airtable Relocations table for all records to find which vendor handled each client move and the vendor cost listed in Special Requirements. Agent discovers vendor assignments (Heartland vs. Swift) per relocation and per-move vendor costs ($1,600 standard per Heartland move). Also discovers the cancelled moves. Lily Marchetti (cancelled Feb 10, Heartland never acknowledged) and Danielle Osei (cancelled Feb 13, Heartland never acknowledged) and Priya Venkatesh (completed by Swift, not Heartland), which are the core of the billing dispute.
OE 3: Search emails for Heartland billing dispute correspondence (queries like "Heartland", "dispute", "overbilling") to find Marcus's formal dispute letter to Jake Loomis, Chloe's cancellation evidence, and the payment hold status. Agent discovers the $4,800 overcharge for 3 illegitimate line items and that the dispute is still unresolved.
OE 4: Search Slack messages for vendor rate increase discussions (queries like "rate increase", "Heartland", "Swift", "vendor") to find: Greg Pallone's Swift 15% base rate increase + $500 DOT documentation surcharge announcement (effective immediately, April 11), Jake Loomis/Heartland tying their 20% Q3 rate increase to the unresolved HM-2026-Q1-0042 billing dispute, Chloe probing Lakeline Logistics as a backup vendor (limited to Midwest and Southeast corridors — cannot serve as national replacement), and Blessing reporting Swift driver pushback on new paperwork. Agent also discovers Alejandro's own margin compression model showing 6-8 point compression.
OE 5: Search Slack #finance channel (via conversations_history or conversations_search_messages) for financial discussions to find: Hana's DOT penalty accrual entry ($50,000 contingent liability, ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001), Marcus's post about the Mosaic prototype damage ($90K value, $50K insurance coverage, $40K gap), Hana's Mosaic credit memo (CM-2026-0415), Marcus's KeyMove piano insurance claim ($1,200 rider), and Marcus's note about the StormCloud $500 credit posted to the wrong QuickBooks estimate.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for invoices, accounts, and accruals to verify the booked amounts. OE 8: Search CRM for deals across active client companies (via crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies) to find contract values and deal amounts per client. Agent discovers deal values needed for margin calculation: Vectral (~$109,900 total, 32% margin), NorthWind ($60,000 enterprise contract, $14,700 Denver expansion), Canopy Health (multiple deals including $9,210 Q2 wave and $12,170 executive relocation), Mosaic ($9,010 April batch), BrightLoop, GreenStack, etc.
OE 9: Search Slack messages for AM exposure mapping responses (queries like "exposure", "client impact", "rate increase") to find what each AM reported after David's request. Agent discovers: Mina flagged Vectral and BrightLoop as most exposed, Emeka flagged Mosaic, and Catalina did not respond (went silent).
OE 10: Search Slack for Hana's reconciliation findings beyond the original Heartland dispute to find that she discovered broader reconciliation gaps during the vendor consolidation crisis (referenced in her persona brief and Slack posts in #finance).
OE 11: Verify and update QuickBooks accrual amounts (via quickbooks_update_account) if any booked amounts don't match the actual current status discovered during investigation e.g., adjusting the Heartland dispute accrual if Hana's additional findings change the disputed total, or updating the DOT accrual if the documentation audit resolved favorably.
OE 12: Look up Marcus Thorne's contact details using contacts_search_contacts to confirm his email address (marcus.thorne@moveops.com) for the email delivery.
OE 13: Post a financial exposure summary in the #finance Slack channel (via conversations_add_message) covering: vendor margin impact analysis by client, total contingent liability rollup across all open items (Heartland dispute, DOT accrual, Mosaic damage, stipend holds, StormCloud credit error, KeyMove piano claim), and Heartland dispute status so Marcus and Hana can validate the numbers.
OE 14: Update Linear tickets tracking financial items (via linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment) with current financial context e.g., the Heartland dispute tracking ticket, DOT compliance tickets, Mosaic damage claim ticket, and stipend investigation ticket (ENG-220).
OE 15: Send email (via send_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com with the full margin analysis (current vendor costs per client, projected margin compression from rate increases, clients most impacted), total contingent liability rollup with verified amounts, and Heartland dispute findings giving Marcus everything he needs for Elena's vendor strategy memo.

8 The prompt asks 'what the new rates do to our margins on each account.' The 25% margin floor is a known threshold established by Elena and Marcus. The analysis must name specific clients affected.
outcome
 ✓✓—✓✓— 100%

9 The email to Marcus references the Heartland billing dispute, identifying at least two of the three contested line items: Lily Marchetti (cancelled move), Danielle Osei (cancelled move), or Priya Venkatesh (move handled by Swift Relocations, not Heartland).
outcome
 ✓✓—✓✓— 100%

✓Run #5The email explicitly identifies all three disputed Heartland line items: Lily Marchetti ('CANCELLED Feb 10'), Danielle Osei ('CANCELLED Feb 13'), and Priya Venkatesh ('Move completed by Swift Relocations, NOT Heartland').

10 The email to Marcus includes a total contingent liability figure or rollup that sums at least three distinct open financial items.
outcome
 ✓✓—✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The email provides a contingent liability rollup with seven distinct items: Mosaic damage ($90K), DOT penalties ($50K), Heartland dispute ($12,800), KeyMove piano ($1,200), stipend holds ($134), Mosaic surcharges ($2,450), Swift surcharges ($2,000) — totaling $158,584.
